item.title: The Founding Father Has Issues With the Office of the Presidency
item.description: He couldn’t get out of political office fast enough.

item.title: You Can’t Defend a Nation When Soldiers Don’t Have Child Care
item.description: Trump wants to pay for the military, not child care. History shows that the armed services are at their best only when soldiers’ kids are cared for.

item.title: The Economic Cost of Donald Trump’s Constant Chaos
item.description: Nonstop political chaos is badly damaging our economy.

item.title: The Town That Reveals All of Trump’s Bad Economic Ideas
item.description: The jobs are coming back, despite President Trump’s tariffs and harsh immigration enforcement.

item.title: No Nation Is Spared in This War
item.description: The Iran war is just the latest setback in the past several years to low-income and lower-middle-income countries.

item.title: The Trump Administration Is Coming After Birth Control Access in a Terrifying New Way
item.description: The new Title X guidance from the Trump administration mentions contraception only once.

item.title: The Four Lessons Liberals Should Consider After Orban’s Defeat
item.description: The lesson for Republicans comes too late, but liberals can still learn from the end of Orbanism.

item.title: The Warmongers Are Getting History All Wrong
item.description: As in Thucydides’ time, the Trump administration’s war posture promises short-term gains but long-run disaster.

item.title: #MeToo and the Fight to End Sex Abuse in Congress
item.description: Readers discuss Eric Swalwell and sexual abuse in Congress. Also: Waning MAGA pride; ice cream and a lap cat as distractions.

item.title: Live Nation. Amazon. Americans Are Standing Up to Corporate Lawbreaking.
item.description: Lina Khan and Doha Mekki on three court rulings that illustrate the power juries and local officials have to enforce antitrust laws.

item.title: Hollywood Movies Like ‘Project Hail Mary’ Are Too Long These Days
item.description: “Project Hail Mary” is just the latest overlong blockbuster.

item.title: Anthropic Wants Claude to Be Moral. Is Religion Really the Answer?
item.description: A.I. cannot meditate, fast or prostrate itself in prayer.

item.title: The Divine Right of Presidents Is a Dangerous Idea
item.description: It didn’t work out too well for kings, either.

item.title: David Zaslav Ate Hollywood
item.description: As the sale of Warner Bros. Discovery to Paramount Skydance hurtles toward board approval, David Zaslav is about to be more than an accidental mogul.

item.title: The Second Coming of Donald Trump
item.description: Who doesn’t get the president mixed up with Jesus?

item.title: JD Vance’s Very Bad Week
item.description: What recent events in Europe can tell us about the future of Trumpism.

item.title: None of This Is Going Putin’s Way
item.description: The war in Iran is taking a huge toll on Russia.

item.title: Times Are Hard. What’s a Teacher to Do?
item.description: Readers respond to an Opinion essay by Frank Bruni. Also: An arch near Arlington; cuts in mental health care; transformation in prison.

item.title: The Mind-Altering Power of Lucid Dreaming
item.description: Few people experience lucid dreams, yet those who do are able to appreciate as much asleep as the rest of us do awake.

item.title: You Can’t Game Your Way to a Real Education
item.description: Technology must return to its proper place in the classroom — as a supplemental tool rather than the source and summit of education.

item.title: Trump Is Fixated on 1896
item.description: The president loves the 19th century. But why?

item.title: The Singular Beauty of Old Ladies
item.description: The old ladies that I know have a grace and stature that only long years of hoping and striving — and living — can bestow.

item.title: The Pope Bedevils Trump
item.description: A higher authority finally tells Trump he’s no messiah.

item.title: Why the Stock Market Makes No Sense Right Now
item.description: It will always be saved.

item.title: How to Relieve Suffering on the Streets of San Francisco
item.description: Readers respond to a guest essay about the city’s recent efforts to reduce drug use and homelessness.

item.title: From Hungary to the Pope, the Right’s Very Bad Week
item.description: What recent events in Europe can tell us about the future of Trumpism.

item.title: Our Tax System Is Bad for America
item.description: Our current tax system isn’t fair, and that is bad for social solidarity, argues the Opinion columnist Ezra Klein. The tax expert Ray Madoff joins him to discuss how a fairer system is a moral necessity.

item.title: A Paris Court Just Rewrote the Rules of Corporate Morality
item.description: The profit motive was on trial. The verdict was scathing.

item.title: Iran Resisted a Powerful Attacker. Taiwan Can, Too.
item.description: By holding off America’s more powerful military, Iran showed how Taiwan can deter or defeat China.

item.title: ‘Salaries Are for Suckers’
item.description: How is it possible that a billionaire like Jeff Bezos has a lower tax rate than the average American? The tax expert Ray Madoff joins the Opinion columnist Ezra Klein to explain how the ultra-wealthy pay a disproportionately low amount of federal income taxes in comparison to their overall wealth.

item.title: It’s the 1970s Again. Democrats Should Study Up on Reagan.
item.description: Good politicians play the hand they’re dealt.

item.title: Section 702 Lets the Government Spy on Americans. Congress Must Fix It.
item.description: It’s time to fix a crucial but flawed tool that has allowed the government to violate Americans’ constitutional rights.

item.title: Orban Lost. But Populism Is Very Much Alive.
item.description: Critics of populists need to do more than thrive off the missteps of their opponents.

item.title: A.I.: Creativity Killer or Companion?
item.description: Readers respond to a column by Ezra Klein. Also: Reflections on Pope Leo XIV; the healing power of nurses in the I.C.U.

item.title: MAHA Propelled Kennedy to the White House. It May Become His Downfall.
item.description: MAHA propelled Robert F. Kennedy Jr. to the White House. It may become his downfall.

item.title: Our Tax System Should Make You Furious
item.description: The tax expert Ray Madoff explains why the American tax system is broken, and how to make it fairer.

item.title: Three Questions for Kevin Warsh, Trump’s Fed Pick
item.description: Senators must assess whether Mr. Warsh would perform his duties as a public servant, or whether he would serve the interests of the president.

item.title: When Good Grammar Gets Snobbish
item.description: Rules don’t always lead to good sense.

item.title: We Are Gambling Away Our Future
item.description: America’s view of liberty is upside down.

item.title: Why Jeff Bezos’ Tax Rate Is Lower Than Yours
item.description: The tax expert Ray Madoff explains why the American tax system is broken, and how to make it fairer.

item.title: Hillary Clinton’s Plan to Help Families
item.description: Readers respond to “How to Fix Affordability,” an Opinion guest essay by Hillary Clinton. Also: President Trump’s arch; protests and litigation.

item.title: This Is Where Donald Trump Loses
item.description: President Trump won’t be able to defy the courts if he loses his Supreme Court case on birthright citizenship, which he is likely to do, the conservative court watcher Sarah Isgur argues on “Interesting Times.”

item.title: The Failure of Donald Trump
item.description: President Trump’s efforts to rule through executive order on issues like tariffs and birthright citizenship have largely failed — and will continue to fail — the conservative court watcher Sarah Isgur argues on “Interesting Times.”

item.title: Trump Is the End of a 100-Year Experiment
item.description: A conservative court watcher explains why the president has failed to bend the judicial branch to his will.

item.title: Older Women Are in Demand by Younger Men
item.description: What a shift in the dating preferences of younger men reveals about our changing norms.

item.title: Trump Is the End of a 100-Year Experiment
item.description: A conservative court watcher explains why the president has failed to bend the judicial branch to his will.

item.title: Why Young Men Want to Be With Older Women
item.description: Age-gap dating is everywhere right now — especially younger men seeking out older women. On “The Opinions,” men share why they’re drawn to the maturity, stability and clarity they can’t find elsewhere.

item.title: When Older Women Date Younger Men
item.description: There’s a growing trend of younger men dating older women — and unlike the backlash older men face, this shift can be perceived as women reclaiming their power, Emily Leibert argues on “The Opinions.”

item.title: Older Women Are in Demand by Younger Men
item.description: What a shift in the dating preferences of younger men reveals about our changing norms.

item.title: What Went Wrong With Criminal Justice Reform
item.description: Americans want a new approach to policing and incarceration.
